title: "String.indexOf" should be used with correct ranges
description: Ensures that calls to String.indexOf are made with valid range arguments to avoid logic errors and potential bugs.
recommendation: Always check that the start index and length arguments for String.indexOf are within the bounds of the string to prevent unexpected results or exceptions.
impact: Incorrect usage of String.indexOf with invalid ranges can lead to incorrect program behavior or runtime exceptions.

compliant:
String s = "example";
int idx = s.indexOf("a", 2); // 2 is a valid start index

checks.message: Always check that the start index and length arguments for String.indexOf are within the bounds of the string to prevent unexpected results or exceptions.
